name: Abby Stanglin
paragraphs: Abby Stanglin draws on 18 years of experience in the intelligence sector in New York and London, most recently at Nardello & Co. Abby has successfully run strategic intelligence projects and complex international litigation support on behalf of sovereign wealth funds, Fortune 500 corporates, hedge funds and government clients. Abby has completed Advanced Covert Surveillance (ACS) training, a programme in International Art Crime Studies and holds an MA in Global Security Studies. She is ranked as a Notable Practitioner in Chambers & Partners Business Intelligence and Investigations Guide 2023.

name: Adam Wilkinson
paragraphs: Adam supports high-profile individuals and leading businesses in countering threats to their privacy, security or reputation. He also provides strategic advice to clients seeking unlock high-stakes opportunities.

Adam’s primary focus is on resolving political and commercial conflicts using diplomacy, intelligence, communications and where necessary, security. An expert in countering and mitigating digital and physical risk, he oversees high-stakes surveillance and complex security tasks across the globe, leading direct negotiations during blackmail, ransomware and extortion cases.

An agile problem-solver with a military background, Adam has an international network and a focus on clients operating in high-risk sectors and challenging markets - at the moment, particularly in Sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Alasdair Drennan
paragraphs: Alasdair Drennan is a strategic communications specialist with over a decade of experience working at the heart of government. In times of crisis, Alasdair excels in providing a quick, methodical response whilst offering a calm and reassuring voice to his clients - who are often in the public eye or under scrutiny.

An expert in developing effective communications strategies in high-stakes situations, Alasdair strategically exploits owned social media channels and other digital platforms to pre-empt adverse media attention and get his clients on the front foot. He is particularly adept at integrating press handling, litigation and digital platforms to create a consistent and compelling approach.

Alasdair’s career has spanned communications and policy roles across various government departments, primarily in the Home Office, where he led the communications response to several terrorist attacks and other major incidents. He has provided strategic advice on communications, policy and operational matters to Cabinet ministers and senior leaders within the Civil Service. Alasdair also holds a postgraduate diploma in strategic communications.

name: Aleksandra Trajkovic
paragraphs: Aleksandra Trajkovic is an experienced intelligence expert, skilled in managing complex and sensitive investigations spanning jurisdictions and involving disputes, litigation support, reputational risk, asset tracing, due diligence and market entry assessments. These necessitate both open and human source intelligence gathering. As a former political advisor in a post-conflict setting, Aleksandra has rich expertise in the nuances and inner workings of politics across jurisdictions, which is invaluable in her specialist work in private diplomacy. Aleksandra is a native speaker of Bosnian-Croatian-Montenegrin-Serbian, speaks fluent Italian, near-fluent French, and has working knowledge of Spanish, Catalan, Macedonian and Slovenian.

name: Alexander Shaw
paragraphs: Alexander Shaw is a global campaigns, communications and issues management specialist with over twelve years’ experience working in multiple markets around the world. He has worked at Edelman and Teneo, including in the Middle East. His expertise lies in crisis and issues management, mega-event planning and delivery, sustainability, international development and geo-political affairs. Alexander has led projects and programmes for a variety of government, corporate and third sector clients, most recently working on COP28 and the Qatar World Cup. He speaks Arabic and Persian.

name: Allan Dunlavy
paragraphs: Allan, who leads our US office, is a trusted adviser to individuals, family offices, and global businesses navigating high-stakes challenges and preparing for opportunities. He has extensive experience building, managing and protecting privacy, reputation, and security in today’s intricate and rapidly evolving digital landscape. In particular, he has a proven history of successfully helping clients unlock strategic opportunities, harness the power of their reputations and position themselves for success.

Allan delivers holistic and practical counsel, particularly to clients in the United States, and Central, Latin and South America, by gaining a deep understanding of their professional and personal lives. With a background in commercial, media, privacy, and IP litigation, he is well-placed to advise on all aspects of reputation, privacy and security, including privacy breaches, false allegations, and campaign, as well as the scrutiny and communications surrounding high-profile litigation.
He is consistently recognised as a leading expert, including in the Chambers & Partners High Net Worth Guide, Spear’s 500, and the Private Client Global Elite.

name: Anna Bloch
paragraphs: She supports her clients to navigate high stakes challenges—protecting what matters most and creating the conditions for long term commercial success. With deep expertise in strategic communications and crisis management, Anna shapes and deploys narratives that resonate in boardrooms, governments and beyond. She has a track record of successfully delivering campaigns across a range of sectors including infrastructure, financial services and tech that secure investment, change policy and build trust where reputation and value are at stake.

name: Aoife Butler-Nolan
paragraphs: Aoife Butler-Nolan is a reputation and corporate communications specialist with over a decade of experience leading on strategic communications for a range of prominent organisations. She advises clients on all aspect of communication strategies including reputation management, political communications, media relations, digital communications, and global campaigns.

Aoife is experienced in advising clients with operations in multiple jurisdictions, including Ireland, the Middle East, Africa, and the US. Her previous roles span the public, private and voluntary sectors, having previously worked for the Department of the Taoiseach, Flutter Entertainment plc and the Greater London Authority. Aoife also holds a postgraduate degree in political communication.

name: Ben Hobbs
paragraphs: Ben Hobbs is a leading reputation and privacy lawyer, drawing on 15 years of litigation experience to help guide FTSE 100 companies and business-owners through high stakes reputational crises and opportunities.

Ben specialises in defamation, privacy, harassment and intellectual property issues. He leads multidisciplinary teams of lawyers, investigators and communications advisers to help clients solve complex problems and navigate to successful strategic outcomes. Ben works between our Dublin and London offices, and is dual-qualified in England & Wales and Ireland.
Ben is ranked amongst the Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index for 2024 in the Spear’s 500, and features in the Citywealth Leaders List.

name: Bota Iliyas
paragraphs: Working at the intersection of intelligence, strategy, and crisis response, she delivers crucial intelligence that informs market assessments, litigation support, and evaluations of complex political and regulatory environments.

With experience across the US, Europe, Central Asia, and pre‑war Russia, Bota provides senior decision-makers with clear insight into how geopolitical developments shape commercial strategy and reputational exposure. A recognised commentator, she has provided analysis for CNBC, Bloomberg, and The New York Times. She holds a master’s degree from Harvard University and is a native Russian speaker.

name: Brett Reynolds
role: Chief Operations Officer and Chief Financial Officer, Partner
paragraphs: Brett Reynolds leads finance, operations, IT, risk management and compliance for Schillings. He is responsible for financial and operational performance and growth.

Previously a board director of a large, fast-growing international media group, Brett brings a wealth of experience driving strategic turnover and profit growth. His responsibilities include selecting, negotiating and integrating acquisitions, evaluating organic growth opportunities and improving operational efficiency, revenue, costs and margins. He has a ‘Big Four’ professional services background and is a Chartered Accountant.

name: Brittany Damora
paragraphs: Brittany Damora advises those with high-risk global exposures, including international families, corporations and governments, on a range of security threats and complex issues related to corruption and transnational repression. Brittany is a kidnap for ransom and counter-extortion specialist with nearly twenty years’ experience working across the globe to resolve a range of complex and high-stakes issues – particularly in Africa and other emerging markets.

name: Caterina Arcuri
paragraphs: She uncovers critical evidence to support cross border investigations, sensitive reputation strategies, and high stakes litigation.

Caterina deploys advanced open-source investigative techniques to assess reputational, privacy, and security risks across regulatory environments. She has particular expertise in detailed due diligence and asset tracing reports, and provides crucial intelligence for corporate investigations and bespoke political risk forecasts.

Caterina has worked for leading investigative firms and political consultancies, supporting institutional clients, investment banks, and companies operating in high-risk sectors. Bilingual in Italian and English, and professionally proficient in Spanish, her work often focuses on Europe and South America.

name: Charlie Lait
paragraphs: Charlie oversees the social media strategy team at Schillings, which sits within our Digital Resilience practice. She builds robust reputational strategies for clients either in crisis or those looking to proactively harness their online profile in pursuit of strategic objectives.

Charlie brings more than ten years' experience in developing and managing the online reputations of high-profile businesses, political leaders and public figures. Prior to joining Schillings, she was Director of Digital for Blackstone, the world’s largest Private Equity firm, where she was responsible for developing the digital reputation of the business and its leaders in EMEA. She previously managed the Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan’s digital communications team, overseeing digital strategy across all areas of digital communications and shaping London's online crisis response protocols for numerous major public safety incidents, as well as the COVID-19 pandemic.

name: Charlotte Finney
paragraphs: Charlotte is an experienced intelligence analyst specialising in open-source investigations and reputational due diligence.
She uncovers critical evidence and insights to support complex investigations and cross-jurisdictional litigation proceedings.

Charlotte is highly skilled in producing detailed privacy exposure reports and conducting open-source research to identify privacy and security risks. She has previously worked in the intelligence industry in the US, which has given her extensive knowledge on US public records and an empirical knowledge of the region.
Charlotte has worked for several leading investigations firms, where she focused on internal corporate investigations, due diligence and asset tracing.

name: Charlotte Watson
paragraphs: Charlotte Watson is a seasoned defamation and privacy lawyer with thirty years’ experience in the reputation, privacy and security environment. She brings a balanced perspective to her work – poacher and gamekeeper – having worked both for claimants and in-house with media organisations. Charlotte leads the Schillings Art Task Force, which focuses on high stakes situations involving art transactions.

name: Chris Bell-Watson
paragraphs: With extensive experience at the intersection of social intelligence, communications and digital strategy, he has advised organisations across the technology, retail, humanitarian and consumer sectors. He is skilled in monitoring fast‑moving news and online narratives, identifying emerging risks, and translating complex data into clear, actionable insight for legal, communications and leadership teams. His work spans real‑time narrative tracking, uncovering the spread of harmful content, and shaping strategic responses to issues as they escalate. Before joining Schillings, Chris spent more than a decade in senior insight roles, working with FTSE 100 and Fortune 500 organisations across sectors.

name: Claire Greaney
paragraphs: Claire Greaney is an experienced media lawyer, providing expert legal advice on defamation, privacy and data protection on Southern Hemisphere time. She is a key part of Schillings' ’‘follow the sun’ 24/7 service model. With a breadth of experience as a commercial litigator, Claire assists high profile individuals and multinational companies on pre- and post-publication matters, as well as pre-emptive reputation and privacy and protection solutions. Claire is admitted to practice as a barrister and solicitor in New Zealand.

name: Claudine Murphy
paragraphs: She is a trusted Solicitor in complex, high-stakes litigation. Most recently, she has worked on a major libel claim against a UK media organisation, and two separate Judicial Review claims against a government body.

Claudine delivers strategic legal responses to media intrusion and reputational threats, working within a multi-disciplinary team of lawyers, investigators, and communications experts. She regularly advises on urgent pre-publication challenges and post-publication remedies, to protect clients’ rights across print, broadcast, and digital media.

Claudine trained at a top-tier Irish law firm and is dual qualified in England & Wales and Ireland. Outside of her legal practice, she is a Champion for Smart Works, a charity dedicated to helping women gain the confidence, clothing, and coaching they need to succeed in job interviews and secure employment.

name: Craig Edwards
paragraphs: As Schillings’ Chief Information Security Officer, Craig Edwards is responsible for enhancing the firm’s cyber and information security. He has nearly 20 years of experience across government, industry and consulting. In addition to his time at a global strategic advisory firm, Craig held senior cyber security roles at the UK’s Government Communications Headquarters (GCHQ) and at a FTSE 100 energy corporate. He is also available to support clients with their digital security needs.

name: David Imison
paragraphs: David Imison leads Schillings and is responsible for the strategic growth of the business as it expands internationally and widens its multidisciplinary operations. He is also an experienced handler of high-stakes situations, having spent ten years working with our clients before assuming the role of Chief Executive Officer.
A former ‘Big Four’ Advisor, David features in the Spear’s 500 as a leading figure in the 2025 Best Reputation Managers Index and is recognised in the Citywealth Leaders List.

name: Elaine Gilchrist
paragraphs: Elaine is an experienced advisory and litigation lawyer specialising in the protection of rights, reputation and information in the media and online. Elaine advises in relation to defamation, malicious falsehood, privacy, breach of confidence, data protection, contempt of court, harassment, passing off and the online infringement of intellectual property rights.

Elaine is skilled in providing pre-publication and post-publication advice and has considerable experience in both private practice and in-house roles. Elaine has advised across a broad range of sectors, including finance, retail, politics, travel, media, sport and entertainment. She has acted for a variety of high-profile individuals, FTSE 100 companies, entrepreneurs and media organisations.

name: Ella Handy
paragraphs: Ella specialises in uncovering and identifying information from open and public sources, often as part of complex due diligence and privacy exposure investigations, or to inform reputation strategies. She also finds crucial evidence in support of legal proceedings.

Ella has an extensive academic background and deep expertise in the Middle East and North Africa. She has a particular focus on international security and unstable political environments, and a nuanced understanding of the cultural and geopolitical landscapes of these regions. Ella speaks conversational French and Arabic.

name: Emily Maister
paragraphs: Emily is a policy and communications expert with over a decade of experience at the frontline of UK politics. A rapid problem solver, she is accustomed to working with senior figures under intense public scrutiny who want to land a message in the short-term, realise ambitions in the long-term, and ensure their reputation is protected.

Emily excels in navigating high-pressure environments, both publicly and behind the scenes. She served as a Special Adviser to Prime Minister Boris Johnson at 10 Downing Street in the Political Unit, briefing the Prime Minister and his Cabinet ahead of all media and public appearances, including Prime Minister’s Questions. Emily also served as Special Adviser to the Secretary of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, Michael Gove, and to the Conservative Party Chairman, Amanda Milling.

In times of crisis, Emily delivers clear, strategic guidance, keeping stakeholders informed and ensuring communications are effectively delivered to both the public and key decision-makers. She is equally adept at realising proactive opportunities for her clients. Her campaign work has been recognised nationally, earning her a ‘Campaign Manager of the Year’ award, having worked on local, mayoral and national election campaigns at the Conservative Campaign Headquarters. She also worked for the Conservative Research Department, before becoming a Special Adviser.

name: Emily Mitcheson
paragraphs: Blending legal strategy with business acumen, she designs strategies that ensure clients are protected in the court of law and the court of public opinion.

Emily’s commercial experience spans digital marketing, energy, retail, sport, and finance. She is trusted to lead high‑stakes, often public‑facing matters, guiding clients through fast‑moving situations across jurisdictions.

Working closely with Schillings’ in-house investigators and communications advisors, she aligns evidence, narrative and legal strategy to deliver holistic and commercial solutions. Emily is recognised by the Legal 500 as a ‘Rising Star’.

name: Emily White
paragraphs: She has a particular focus on cases with a geo-political context, including transnational repression and navigating conflict and post conflict situations, and has experience assisting clients operating in East and West Africa and other emerging markets.

name: Evgenia Ashworth
paragraphs: Evgenia is a highly skilled reputation and privacy lawyer with a background in commercial litigation. Having worked on some of the highest profile civil fraud cases in the UK, she is particularly adept at advising clients on how to navigate the reputational impact of such major litigation, especially when it receives significant press attention. This often includes post-publication media engagement, as well as implementing privacy protection strategies, and working on resilience building in the aftermath. Evgenia also works closely with intelligence and digital experts on litigation support, especially with regards evidence gathering, and rebuttals of inaccurate reporting. Evgenia is dual-qualified in England & Wales and Russia.

name: Filip Sys
paragraphs: His practice includes urgent pre-publication engagement and securing post publication remedies across print, broadcast, and digital media, as well as protecting clients’ personal data. Filip’s practice is grounded in commercial litigation and strengthened by experience in high profile disputes and internal investigations. Filip is a Founding Member of the Gypsy, Roma and Traveller Lawyers Association in the United Kingdom.

name: Filipa Macedo
paragraphs: Filipa is a skilled senior intelligence analyst and researcher with a background in social media investigations and due diligence. She specialises in gathering intelligence from open and public sources, often as part of privacy exposure investigations to proactively help clients pre-empt threats to their privacy and reputation, and also to find crucial information in support of complex investigations and litigation. Often there is a geopolitical dimension to these investigations, and here, Filipa’s knowledge and understanding of the specific challenges and opportunities affecting Portuguese-speaking countries and MENA region is highly valuable.
Filipa speaks fluent Portuguese, with a working knowledge of Spanish and French.

name: George Pascoe-Watson
paragraphs: George Pascoe-Watson is a founding partner of Schillings Communications LLP.

George is a former Political Editor of The Sun newspaper and Chairman at Portland Communications. He has deep experience as a strategic adviser to high profile individuals, sporting and political leaders, and the boards and C-Suite of some of the world’s largest companies and organisations. These span the defence, pharmaceuticals, transport, major infrastructure, regulated utilities, professional services, FMCG and energy sectors. George draws on a wide network in the media, politics, and corporate life, as well as years of strategic communications experience. He is a seasoned broadcaster and regularly appears as a political commentator on UK television and radio.

George is featured in the Spear’s 500 2025 Best Reputation Managers Index and has been recognised, jointly with Victoria O'Byrne, as the number one advisor for crisis and reputation management in PR Week's 2025 Power Book. George has also featured in the Mace Top 100 Political Consultants Index 2025.

name: Gillian Duffy
paragraphs: Drawing on her background as a litigator and expertise in libel and privacy law, she helps international companies and ultra-high-net-worth families handle high stakes situations. These include protecting her clients from orchestrated smear campaigns, mass data leaks such as the Panama Papers and Paradise Papers, helping navigate legal and reputational challenges in new markets and defending against false and inaccurate media reporting.

Gillian is recognised as a key practitioner in the Legal 500 Reputation Management guide and is listed in the Spear’s 500 Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index for 2024. She is also ranked in the Citywealth Leaders List.

name: Giulia Valeri
paragraphs: Giulia Valeri is an experienced intelligence gatherer and investigator with a track record of successful open source investigations , conducting due diligence, hostile actor research and asset valuations and tracing operations. Giulia speaks Italian, Mandarin Chinese and Spanish.

name: Harriet Black
paragraphs: Harriet is an experienced integrated communications consultant specialising in reputation management for global corporates and individuals in the public eye. Previously Director at communications agency, Portland, Harriet has spent nearly a decade advising high-profile clients and well-known brands across a range of sectors - including retail, FMCG, energy and technology - to seize strategic opportunities, handle crises and navigate high stakes moments.

Adept at both proactive profile-building as well as crisis response work, Harriet has particular expertise in developing and executing large-scale multi-stakeholder campaigns for international corporates. During periods of significant change and operational risk, she supports clients through strategically building narratives and engagement plans to influence political stakeholders, investors and the media – whilst ensuring that their reputation is protected.

name: Hind Habib
paragraphs: Trusted by some of the world’s most successful individuals, businesses, and family offices, Hind is known for her incisive legal advice and robust litigation strategies that safeguard and enhance reputations in the highest-stakes situations. Clients turn to Hind for discreet, decisive counsel – whether they are seeking to exploit major commercial opportunities or during times of crisis, when businesses and livelihoods are on the line.
Her expertise spans defamation, privacy, confidentiality, harassment, intellectual property, and contractual disputes. Hind has been recognised as a Rising Star in the Private Client Global Elite.

name: Hope Watson
paragraphs: Hope Watson is an experienced intelligence specialist with recent experience of large, complex and pan-jurisdictional litigation, involving complex open source intelligence collection, rigorous analysis and network mapping. Hope has also conducted witness identification and interviewing, both in the UK and overseas, and worked on trial preparation, witness profile reports and the deep analysis of disclosure documents.

name: Jack Ryan
paragraphs: Jack Ryan is an intelligence consultant who works on a range of investigations for clients in high stakes situations. He has particular experience in the Middle East and in both Islamist and Far Right terrorism, as well as geo-political analysis. He has worked as a qualitative consultant within the alternative assets sector, conducting due diligence and reputation studies for several private equity and infrastructure clients. Jack has worked in the Afghanistan and Pakistan Studies department at Washington DC’s Middle East Institute, where he covered analysis of regional fiscal policy, security and trade.

name: Jamie Fenton
paragraphs: He advises prominent individuals and international organisations on high stakes matters, navigating complex crises and capitalising on transformative opportunities.

Jamie supports clients on every aspect of strategic communications, including global campaigns, media relations, crisis response, and corporate affairs. He designs and leads complex, high-profile communications strategies that influence the audiences that matter around the world.

Today, Jamie is a trusted advisor to clients in entertainment and finance. With extensive experience in sports communications, has worked on some of the most prestigious international tournaments with leading teams and clubs. He’s also advised some of the biggest stars in the sporting world, advising on reputation and strategic positioning.

name: Jason Frydman
paragraphs: Jason is an experienced lawyer who specialises in defamation and privacy law and commercial litigation. This blend of experience in commercial and media litigation is invaluable in working with clients who need to protect their reputations in a complicated business context. Jason is also qualified in Australia.

name: Jenna Goldberg
paragraphs: Jenna Goldberg is a strategic communications and corporate affairs specialist with almost 20 years’ experience leading campaigns, managing reputations and advising on issues and crises. She works with both global corporations and private individuals; partnering with leaders to enhance their profiles and build confidence in their public personas.

Jenna has worked with clients in the FMCG, professional services and energy sectors and spent many years in real estate and infrastructure; convening decisionmakers, influencers and communities to deliver sensitive but significant change for clients. She has delivered campaigns and provided strategic counsel for major developers, investors, place-makers and the public sector, including the NHS.

Jenna joined Schillings from global PR firm, Burson and before that was a Partner and Managing Director of Insight at a specialist built environment consultancy. She is also a trustee of the Centre for London, a think tank focused on the future of the capital.

name: Jenny Afia
paragraphs: Jenny Afia heads Schillings’ global Entertainment group, practising in privacy, defamation and media disputes. Most files land with publicity already in motion—interim applications, parallel publications, or regulatory questions that will not wait for a final determination on the merits.

Advisory work spans defence, containment, and judicial remedies where the harm is what appears in print or online: takedown and restraint where proportionate, and cross-border coordination when the same material is replicated across jurisdictions or platforms.

Outside litigation she contributes to policy on children’s online safety and digital rights—the UK Children’s Commissioner’s Digital Task Force, the 5Rights report “Disrupted Childhood”, and the evidence base behind the Age-Appropriate Design Code. She is a trustee of Common Sense Media UK.

name: Jessica Boyce
paragraphs: Jessica works for corporates, charities and individuals – both in and out of the spotlight – to advise on matters affecting their reputation and privacy. She is particularly experienced in areas of defamation, malicious falsehood, harassment and data protection law, especially when it concerns high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals. Jessica often supports clients with pre-emptive advice surrounding their reputation and has extensive litigation experience on reputational issues.

name: Jessica Wotton
paragraphs: Jessica Wotton is a solicitor specialising in defamation, privacy and data protection, often advising global organisations and corporates. She has a background in IP and media law, with expertise working on copyright and trademark disputes, as well as complex commercial litigation. Jessica was previously Associate to a Judge of the Federal Court of Australia, which involved assisting in court, reviewing the parties’ evidence and drafting judgments. Jessica is qualified as a solicitor in Australia.

name: Joe Mooney
paragraphs: Joe is a senior intelligence specialist with a focus on Central Asia. He works on a wide range of cross-jurisdictional intelligence projects including litigation support, asset tracing, corruption exposés, and reputation protection for successful individuals and businesses. Having lived and studied in Eastern Europe for several years, Joe is fluent in Russian and has working proficiency in Ukrainian and Polish.

name: Joelle Rich
paragraphs: Joelle focuses on protecting our clients during public crises that threaten unwarranted intrusion, reputation risk and media attention.

She supports talent and other prominent clients and families, especially those based in the US, where she has extensive experience devising bespoke reputation strategies to navigate high-stakes situations.

Joelle is recognised in the Spear’s 500 Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index for 2025 and has been recognised in Citywealth’s Future Leaders Awards and is ranked in the Chambers& Partners 2026 Defamation/Reputation Management guide.

name: John Curtin
paragraphs: John Curtin is a senior media lawyer with substantial experience managing the reputational aspects of business-critical issues for both prominent individuals and international companies. Having previously worked as a commercial litigation solicitor, John now specialises in pre-emptive reputation strategies and managing crises. Dual qualified in Ireland and England & Wales, John is primarily based in our Dublin office.

name: Josh Leigh
paragraphs: Josh Leigh leads the Schillings digital resilience practice. This unit provides proactive and reactive projects to improve and strengthen clients’ online reputations as reflected in search results, on social media and via AI. An expert in multi-territory digital resilience strategies, Josh has an industry-wide reputation for creative and scientific approaches – combined with communications skills – to digital profile challenges, using the latest techniques. Josh previously worked at communication consultancies Hawthorn, Digitalis, Edelman and Havas.
He is ranked in the prestigious Spear’s Best Reputation & Privacy Managers Index 2025.

name: Juliet Young
paragraphs: Regarded as one of the leading investigative experts amongst her peers, Juliet provides strategic counsel and support in relation to crisis management and litigation decisions. She has a background in complex investigations across multiple jurisdictions in support of litigation and is adept at knowing where to collect crucial information and how best to use it to our clients’ advantage.
She also leads the firm's research into synthetic media and junk news.

Juliet was awarded the prestigious Band 1 ranking by Chambers & Partners for their 2025 Litigation Support guide. She features as a leading Reputation Manager in the Spear’s 500 and the Private Client Global Elite Directory for 2025.

name: Karl van der Plas
paragraphs: Karlis an experienced reputation lawyer, providing expert legal advice onprivacy, defamation and commercial disputes.

In addition to advising on pre- and post-publication matters and proactive privacy protection, he has acted in a broad spectrum of commercial cases, including class actions and regulatory proceedings. He has a strong track record of guiding clients through the reputational challenges that accompany high-profile litigation and investigations.

Karl is admitted as a Barrister and Solicitor in New Zealand. He has extensive in-court experience which allows Karl to offer clients practical and forward-thinking guidance on how disputes and their reputational elements may play out.
Karl advises on Southern Hemisphere time and is a key part of Schillings ’‘follow the sun" 24/7 service model.

name: Keith Schilling
paragraphs: Keith Schilling founded Schillings in 1984. He is regarded as an eminent practitioner in the fields of reputation and privacy law, credited with creating a right of privacy in England following a landmark ruling by the House of Lords in 2004. With a long history in libel, defamation, privacy, data protection and commercial litigation, Keith’s strategic and visionary counsel is sought by many international corporations and high profile individuals.
Keith is ranked as a leader in his field in Chambers & Partners, the Spear’s 500 Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index, and the Citywealth Leaders List.
Read Keith's Founder's Profile here.

name: Lauren Arthur-Davies
paragraphs: For more than a decade, Lauren Arthur-Davies has provided strategic reputation consultancy to clients across a range of sectors, spanning retail, transport, technology and FMCG. She is an expert in issues and crisis management, in-depth and qualitative research, narrative development, communication strategies, insight and proactive campaigning. Lauren was previously at Teneo and Fishburn Hedges. She has run a UK press office for a social media client facing intense stakeholder scrutiny. She also worked in Brussels for ‘Europe’s World,’ a current affairs magazine by the think tank, Friends of Europe. Lauren is a trained focus group moderator.

name: Lauren Dye
paragraphs: With deep expertise in search engines and generative AI, Lauren provides strategic analysis of search landscapes to guide digital campaigns and inform reputation management strategies.

Lauren has nearly a decade of experience in SEO, much of it within the financial services sector, and brings a wealth of insight and precision to her work, particularly across highly competitive and regulated markets.

name: Lauren Ekblom
paragraphs: Lauren Ekblom (née Barr) is an experienced intelligence expert, specialising in conducting technical open-source investigations with complex data analysis, producing high quality intelligence reports for senior decision makers. An ex-British Army officer, Lauren has also worked for NATO and in government intelligence roles; previously leading a threat analysis team in the multi-agency London situation centre. She is an expert in crisis and incident management.

name: Leila Khaze
paragraphs: Leila Khaze is an experienced lawyer, specialising in helping individuals and families in the public eye to protect their privacy and reputation, both proactively and in times of crisis. This work covers cases involving defamation, adverse media attention, stalking, blackmail, and harassment. With a background in commercial and banking litigation, Leila is a skilled litigator with international experience working with high profile individuals and businesses. Leila is a fluent Farsi speaker. She is also qualified in Australia and as a barrister in Canada.

name: Leyla Najafli
paragraphs: Leyla has led social media strategy across BBC language services in Africa, Asia, and Europe, setting the direction across all major digital platforms. Leyla previously headed brand marketing for a fitness tech start-up, focusing on content strategy, influencer activations and integrated marketing campaigns.

At Schillings, Leyla supports clients in optimising their digital and social media channels to build resilient reputations, ensuring their profiles reflect their values and narrative. Sitting within the social media strategy team of the Schillings’ Digital Resilience Practice, Leyla’s expertise includes channel optimisation, awareness campaigns, and tailoring content for international audiences. Leyla is fluent in Russian and Azerbaijani.

name: Lily Kennett
paragraphs: Lily Kennett draws on a rich background that spans investigative journalism and over a decade in corporate intelligence and investigations.

She works closely with business leaders and prominent individuals to inform strategy during high-stakes situations and advises on remediation and long-term approaches to reputation for those in the public eye. She spearheads Schillings’ pro bono programme, which supports a range of NGOs and individuals working to counter online abuse as well as misinformation and disinformation campaigns online.

Lily received a prestigious Band 2 ranking in Chambers & Partners Litigation Support Guide 2025 as a key practitioner in her field. She is also a commended Reputation Manager in the Spear’s 500 2025 – the ‘bible’ of ultra-high-net-worth advisers - and is recognised in Citywealth’s Leaders List.

name: Lily Wowk
paragraphs: Lily Wowk is an experienced solicitor with a background in international law and arbitration. She specialises in advising clients on global reputation and privacy matters, including pre- and post-publication media response. She regularly works on high-profile litigation involving issues of data protection, defamation and malicious falsehood. Lily is a member of the Data Protection Committee and is contributing to a book on media law and journalism, which is due to be published in 2025.

name: Linda Gu
paragraphs: Linda Gu is a lawyer helping individuals and corporations manage reputational risks and challenges. She has experience in reputation advisory and understands the needs of clients during times of crisis. She has worked with corporate and private clients across a wide range of sectors including finance, property and real estate, tech, and consumer industries. Linda is admitted to practice as a barrister and solicitor in New Zealand.

name: Lord Browne of Madingley
paragraphs: Lord Browne was Group Chief Executive of BP plc from 1995-2007. He was knighted in 1998 and made a crossbench life peer in 2001. He is currently Chairman of BeyondNetZero, a climate growth equity venture. He is also Chairman of The Courtauld Institute of Art, Chairman of the Francis Crick Institute, Chairman of the Queen Elizabeth Prize for Engineering, and Co-Chairman of the Prime Minister’s Council on Science and Technology.

Lord Browne is the Chairman of the John Browne Charitable Trust which supports a wide range of causes in the arts, science and engineering, education, the environment and human rights. He is also the Chairman of the Beaufort Society, the legacy society of St John’s College, Cambridge, and is a past Chair of the Cambridge Judge Business School.

name: Louise Prince
paragraphs: Louise is a senior media lawyer with extensive experience advising clients on a broad range of reputational and privacy issues, specialising in defamation, privacy, and data protection. She represents a diverse client base, including high profile individuals and prominent figures in the business world, both in and out of the public eye. Louise also works with high net worth individuals, as well as leading corporates and brands, to protect their rights and reputations. Louise is ranked by both Chambers UK and Chambers High Net Worth as a Star Associate, where she is commended as ‘a great technical lawyer’ with ‘excellent insight’ and ‘fantastic attention to detail’. She is additionally recognised as a Leading Associate and Key Lawyer by the Legal 500.

name: Luke Kibblewhite
paragraphs: Luke is a strategic lawyer who specialises in data protection, reputation management and privacy. He has a background in public law and commercial litigation and has acted for high profile individual and institutional clients on a wide range of litigation matters. Luke is qualified as a barrister and solicitor in New Zealand.

name: Madeleine Moore
paragraphs: Madeleine Moore is a senior intelligence analyst and investigator, with a range of open source intelligence collection and research skills optimised to find crucial information to help our clients make decisions in high stakes situations, especially with political dimensions, and those with the Middle East and North Africa in the background. Madeleine is fluent in Arabic, Farsi and French.

name: Mark Phillips
paragraphs: Mark is an experienced corporate communicator with over 30 years’ experience leading communications for some of the UK’s most high-profile companies. He specialises in advising leaders at Board and ExCo levels on corporate affairs, government relations and sustainability, looking to mitigate risk, protect reputation, and ensure opportunities are realised.

Mark has worked extensively across Europe, North America, the Middle East and Australia, for businesses spanning defence and security, energy, financial services and aviation sectors. He has managed company reputation and external affairs operations across global markets, acting as a trusted advisor to leadership during all parts of business cycles from rapid growth and market entries to contraction and market and country exits. Mark is an experienced leader of crisis responses.

name: Mark Tibbs
paragraphs: Mark specialises in leading global investigations into smear campaigns, blackmail, and misinformation. His work spans reputational risk, fraud cases, due diligence, asset tracing, and multi-jurisdictional and complex disputes. With advanced experience of cryptocurrency tracing, he has overseen and successfully supported several cases through legal proceedings.

Mark is widely recognised for his ability to unmask - and hold to account -harassers, extortionists, and fraudsters. His investigative career began at the Serious Organised Crime Agency (SOCA) and the National Crime Agency (NCA), including a secondment with the FBI in Washington, D.C., where he gained extensive experience dismantling transnational organised crime networks. More recently, Mark rose to become a Partner in a well-established city law firm, leading their complex investigations team.
Mark holds a Master’s degree in Crime Investigation.

name: Matthew Denton
paragraphs: Matthew is a skilled lawyer who specialises in reputation management, privacy and defamation cases. With experience working for claimants and defendants, Matthew also has a background in intellectual property, public law and commercial litigation. Matthew is also qualified as a solicitor in New Zealand.

name: Matthew Powell
paragraphs: Matthew Powell is a member of Schillings’ executive team, responsible for delivering a range of projects in support of our growth and acquisition strategy. He leads on key aspects of the firm’s marketing and client service approach, as well as the integration of our expanding set of capabilities.

Matthew’s experience spans the corporate world, private equity, family offices and the public sector. He was chief of staff to Lord Browne of Madingley for almost a decade, and part of the team which founded BeyondNetZero, a multibillion-dollar private equity fund focussed on climate solutions.

name: Meg Lawrence
paragraphs: Meg is an expert in paid media advertising - delivering high-performing, multi-platform campaigns that influence policy, increase share of voice and market, and lead to real-world audience behaviours. Meg is also highly experienced in creating and transforming social media channels - from strategic planning through to content creation and performance analytics.

Meg works within the social media strategy team of Schillings’ Digital Resilience practice. She combines organic and paid social strategies to help clients manage and enhance their online profiles, whether publicly or discreetly. Her expertise spans profile optimisation, paid and organic social media, and integrated campaign delivery to help clients unlock strategic opportunities and protect their licence to operate.

name: Michael Mpofu
paragraphs: Michael is a strategic communications adviser with deep expertise in financial services and political communications. He has advised clients across multiple jurisdictions on high stakes matters including transactions, crisis response, media engagement, and reputation management.

Michael has worked internationally, including in South Africa and the UK, supporting senior leaders and institutions through complex and sensitive communications challenges. His experience spans listed companies, as well as hedge funds, private equity firms, high-net-worth individuals, and cross-border mandates.
Before moving into advisory, Michael served as the spokesperson for the Premier of the Western Cape Government and was previously a political journalist based in South Africa’s Parliament.

name: Michaela Janu
paragraphs: Michaela Janu is an experienced lawyer specialising in reputation, privacy and data protection matters. She provides advice on pre- and post- publication media enquiries and articles, misuse of private information, data breaches, intellectual property and media litigation. She has worked on many large, complex multidisciplinary matters. She has a strong complementary background in commercial law. Michaela is also admitted to practice as a solicitor in Australia.

name: Mili Shah
paragraphs: Mili Shah specialises in legal advice in privacy, defamation and data protection for private clients and large corporate organisations, with experience assisting on some of the largest and most high-profile cases in the firm. Combined with her background in commercial litigation, Mili is a highly skilled solicitor who is passionate about protecting clients’ privacy and reputation. She has a personal interest in helping young people enter into the legal profession and is a mentor for students who are interested in a career in law.

name: Natasha Hibbert
paragraphs: Natasha Hibbert is a senior media lawyer with a wealth of experience advising clients in relation to pre- and post-publication matters and all aspects of defamation, privacy, confidence, harassment and data protection. These legal skills are deployed on multidisciplinary mandates for a wide range of clients, including corporates, financial institutions, brands, family offices and individuals, both in and out of the public spotlight. Natasha is recognised in the Legal 500.

name: Nick Brough
paragraphs: Nick Brough practices as a lawyer advising on pre- and post-publication media engagement, privacy, data protection and other legal services relevant to the protection of reputation, privacy and security. Originally a criminal defence solicitor, his broad experience of handling major criminal work, including robustly defending clients in court, gives him an edge in advising clients on their high stakes threats and opportunities.

name: Pavel Trosin
paragraphs: With more than 15 years’ experience in insights, analytics and crisis measurement, Pavel has built and run programmes across technology, financial services, healthcare and media. Before joining Schillings, he held leadership roles at companies including Onclusive, Kekst and Consulum, following senior analytics posts at Edelman.

He has advised on high‑stakes issues for global brands including Google, Microsoft, Nissan, Unilever and PwC. A pragmatic technologist, he integrates AI‑enabled methods into everyday workflows and coaches teams to use advanced tools responsibly. He holds an MSc in Public Affairs & Lobbying and further training in statistical learning and data analysis.

name: Peter Wilson
paragraphs: Peter Wilson specialises in working at pace with senior clients and their teams to handle complex and controversial issues, as well as developing and implementing long-term reputational strategy.

High stakes situations have been at the heart of Peter’s career for more than two decades. His background in government communications spans a range of departments and sectors, including home affairs, energy and climate change, and transport. Peter advised ministers and senior officials under successive governments on major campaigns, media relations, crisis communication, and stakeholder management.

After leading several communications teams in the UK Civil Service, Peter served as Special Adviser to the Secretary of State in three government departments, including international development, education, and health and social care.
Outside government, Peter has provided senior counsel to high profile individuals and public and private sector organisations in the UK, the Middle East and North America.

name: Phil Hartley
paragraphs: Phil Hartley is a leading media lawyer and litigator, with extensive experience in defamation, privacy law, data protection, counter-harassment, confidentiality and intellectual property. Phil has a particular interest, and a depth of expertise, in complex and contentious data privacy cases. His legal work is set within the context of multidisciplinary approaches to high stakes threats and opportunities.

Phil is recognised in the 2024 Spear’s 500 Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index, and is nominated for Lawyer of the Year – Reputation in Spear’s 2024 Awards. He has been recognised in the prestigious Private Client Global Elite directory for 2025. Phil is dual-qualified in England & Wales and Ireland and works in both our London and Dublin offices.

name: Rachel Atkins
paragraphs: Rachel is a leading lawyer specialising in reputation and privacy. Acting for private clients, corporates, and entrepreneurs from the UK and around the world, Rachel is called on in high stakes situations to manage complex cases and the strong personalities involved. She is an experienced litigator, applying calmness, balance and objectivity to her cases. Rachel is often asked to lead corporate internal enquiries, particularly where public scrutiny is intense.

Rachel is featured in Chambers’ Defamation and Reputation Management directory and recognised by Legal 500 as a leading individual. She is ranked in the prestigious Spear’s Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index 2025, and features in the Citywealth Leaders List. She is also amongst the winners of the 2024 Citywealth Powerwomen Awards, and is ranked Tier 1 in the Private Client Global Elite Directory.

name: Rachel Ibbetson
paragraphs: Rachel is a highly skilled intelligence consultant with a background in maritime and international trade. She works on a range of matters for clients in high stakes situations, conducting detailed research to provide crucial evidence for global investigations, with particular emphasis on those concerning maritime matters. Rachel has experience as a commercial shipping analyst focusing on the global seaborne oil trade, and also as an analyst on civil and defence maritime markets for the UK government.

name: Rod Christie-Miller
paragraphs: Rod Christie-Miller is an experienced litigator with decades of work in the areas of law related to reputation, privacy and security. He has acted for a wide range of corporations, brands, governments, and public figures, with a particular focus on the intersection of international business, politics, and geopolitical risks. He has deep expertise assisting clients operating across Africa and in other emerging markets. Rod is ranked as a leader in his field in Chambers & Partners and the Spear’s 500 Power List.
Rod is the Executive Chair of Schillings.

name: Sacha Levine
paragraphs: She advises clients through fast‑moving crises as well as on proactive strategies to prevent issues before they arise. Much of her work focuses on pre‑ and post‑publication matters, providing trusted advice in response to intrusive or inaccurate reporting.

Sacha trained and qualified at the BBC, giving her first‑hand insight into how major media organisations operate. As a result, she has a rare dual perspective, with experience acting on both claimant and defendant matters.

name: Sam Ahuja
paragraphs: Sam Ahuja is an experienced litigator whose legal work covers a range of defamation, privacy and intellectual property issues. He brings that legal expertise into broad multidisciplinary strategies to help handle the high stakes situations faced by our clients. Sam has also served as in-house counsel to a challenger bank.
Sam is ranked in the Citywealth Leaders List, the Spear’s 500 Best Reputation & Privacy Lawyers Index, and was previously listed in the Super Lawyers Directory.

name: Sarah Alawi
paragraphs: Sarah is a privacy and reputation lawyer skilled at helping successful individuals, families, companies and charitable organisations navigate high stakes opportunities and threats. She has a commercial litigation background, having previously worked at a magic circle law firm, a specialist boutique litigation firm and as a judge's clerk at the High Court of New Zealand.
Sarah is a qualified lawyer in England & Wales, Australia (New South Wales) and New Zealand. She holds an LLM from Harvard Law School as a Fulbright Scholar.

name: Sarah Reynolds
paragraphs: Sarah Reynolds specialises in reputation and crisis management with her work covering defamation, privacy, data protection and intellectual property matters. She provides pre- and post-publication advice to high profile individuals and international organisations and assists our clients in protecting their private and confidential information. Sarah is dual-qualified in England and Wales and Scotland and has experience in dealing with various intra-UK and cross-border privacy and reputation matters.

name: Sian Akerman
paragraphs: Siân Akerman is a seasoned media litigator experienced in handling high-profile defamation trials and complex cases. She specialises in pre- and post-publication advice, harassment cases, and contempt of court applications, and has a strong background in commercial litigation and civil fraud. Sian sits on the committee of the Junior London Solicitors Litigation Association.

name: Simon Brown
paragraphs: He helps high-profile individuals, family offices and global corporates navigate high-stakes situations, including pre- and post-publication media issues, online reputational threats and unlocking major opportunities.

A skilled litigator with a track record in the High Court and Court of Appeal, Simon has extensive experience in defamation, privacy, data protection and harassment complaints. He works closely with Schillings’ multidisciplinary experts to develop holistic strategies that protect clients in both the court of law and the court of public opinion.

Beyond crisis response, Simon provides long-term strategic counsel to his often international clients, creating pre-emptive reputation plans and helping them achieve their objectives. He is recognised as a key practitioner in the Legal 500 guide.

name: Simon Curran
paragraphs: Simon’s practice provides pragmatic legal response to reputational risk, working across multidisciplinary teams to manage escalation and secure the removal of harmful content. He has advised on privacy, IP and commercial litigation strategy, supported global investment firms on reputation management, and assisted with data breach and leak response in cross‑border transactions.

Simon trained at a leading corporate law firm, where he gained experience across M&A, IPOs and secondary fundraisings. Outside of his legal practice, he has coordinated and mentored on the THRIVE programme, a social mobility initiative focused on improving socio‑economic diversity within the legal profession.

name: Simon Harris
paragraphs: Simon Harris is a strategic communications specialist with expertise in global campaigns, issues handling and crisis management.

Simon provides discreet advice to high-profile individuals and international businesses, helping clients to unlock opportunities and resolve high-stakes situations. He excels in developing tailored reputation strategies, both in peacetime and in times of crisis, to ensure his clients are protected from threats and positioned for success.

Simon joined Schillings Communications from Visa, the global payments leader, where he helped manage corporate communications across Europe. Prior to this, he worked at strategic communications firms Hanbury Strategy and Hawthorn Advisors, supporting well-known businesses and individuals.

name: Sir George Buckley
paragraphs: Chairman of the Board of Directors at Smiths Group plc, the global technology company, Sir George was formerly Chairman and CEO of Brunswick Corporation and latterly Chairman and CEO of 3M. In addition to his appointment to the Schillings advisory board, Sir George is also a Chairman of Stanley Black & Decker Inc, as well as a Director of PepsiCo Inc and Hitachi Ltd.

name: Sir Mark Lyall Grant GCMG
paragraphs: The UK’s ambassador to the United Nations between 2009 and 2015, Sir Mark went on to become National Security Adviser to Prime Ministers David Cameron and Theresa May. In that role, he was head of the National Security Secretariat and Secretary to the National Security Council.

name: Steven Hudson
paragraphs: With long experience working in all aspects of media law, Steven Hudson specialises in defamation, privacy and data protection. He primarily helps clients in relation to disputes concerning digital, social and traditional media. Steven works with high profile individuals and businesses to defend their reputation and privacy either in moments of crisis or on a more pre-emptive basis, where long-term results rather than quick fixes are required. Steven is listed in the Legal 500 Reputation Management Index as a Rising Star and Next Generation Partner.

name: Tim Robinson CBE
paragraphs: Tim Robinson helps our clients handle high stakes situations where the background is especially complex, large-scale, sensitive and international.

He is an experienced negotiator and transactional adviser and is frequently asked to help settle disputes and lead internal enquiries. He is often called upon to run counter-extortion operations and to deal with smear campaigns. As a former general in the British Army, Tim is a proven battlefield leader, as well as an experienced strategist and tactician in the corporate and private client worlds.
Tim features in the Spear’s Best Reputation Managers Index.

name: Victoria Anderson
paragraphs: Victoria is an experienced lawyer specialising in reputation management, and defamation and privacy law. Victoria is adept at providing incisive pre and post-publication advice, crafting pre-emptive solutions to counter imminent media threats, managing complex crises for our clients and litigating to assert their rights. Victoria is also the Chief Executive Officer of Big Voice London (BVL), a youth empowerment charity, which seeks to give young people from non-fee paying schools a voice in law and legal policy with the aim of improving social mobility in the legal profession. The charity is supported by, but independent from, the UK Supreme Court.

name: Victoria O’Byrne
paragraphs: Victoria O’Byrne is a founding partner of Schillings Communications LLP.

Victoria draws upon over 20 years’ experience in senior positions across UK business and government, previously working for the Royal Family and Sir Richard Branson as Group Director of Corporate Affairs at Virgin. Previously she was a Special Adviser to a UK Cabinet Minister and a Director of Communications for the London 2012 Olympic and Paralympic Games. A highly respected leader in strategic communications, Victoria specialises in working with high profile and highly scrutinised individuals, organisations and campaigns to build their reputations and grow their coalitions of support.

Victoria is featured in the Spear’s 500 2025 Best Reputation Managers Index and has been recognised, jointly with George Pascoe-Watson, as the number one advisor for crisis and reputation management in PR Week's 2025 Power Book.

name: Victoria Vasileva
paragraphs: Victoria Vasileva is highly skilled researcher with a background in conducting complex investigations, often where high-risk jurisdictions are involved. These investigations typically identify threats and networks, locate people or assets and carry out due diligence, including where criminality is at play. Victoria is native in Russian and fluent Greek.

name: Viv O’Connor-Jemmett
paragraphs: Viv O’Connor-Jemmett is a Consultant Partner of Schillings International LLP, based in Schillings’ Dublin office, supporting clients in the public eye to build and protect their profiles online– while maintaining their right to privacy.

Having held senior digital roles at several global communications agencies, Viv has developed a unique blend of pioneering digital and communications skills that effectively protect his clients’ interests during periods when their reputation is under threat. He has particular expertise in helping clients sensitively and discreetly navigate highly complex matters, often during times of crisis, working to rapidly mitigate threats as they emerge.

Viv also develops bespoke strategies to help his clients proactively shape their own narrative, build resilience and harness opportunity, through sophisticated digital campaigns. With over 15 years’ experience in Digital Resilience, Viv has advised boards and CEOs of global corporations, high-profile individuals and their families, in addition to governments and royalty.

name: Will Lowe
paragraphs: Will Lowe is an experienced intelligence specialist, with notable expertise in complex international and multi-jurisdictional investigations: these often involve open-source investigations, due diligence reports and human source enquiries. Will specialises in counter-disinformation and counter-smear campaigns – particularly online – and is especially adept in collecting and analysing intelligence drawn from social media. He speaks Mandarin Chinese and has a nuanced understanding of the cultural and geopolitical context of East Asia.

name: Zahra Gray
paragraphs: Zahra is a senior intelligence analyst specialising in the Middle East and North Africa. With a background in management consulting, corporate intelligence, and stakeholder due diligence, she helps clients understand risks to privacy and security and advises on strategies to mitigate these.

Zahra is highly skilled at gathering intelligence and uncovering crucial evidence from open and public sources, and notably social media (SOCMINT), either as part of privacy exposure investigations or in support of litigation. She has deep expertise in complex corporate mapping and asset tracing. Zahra frequently supports clients operating in the Middle East, Gulf Regions and has a nuanced understanding of the cultural and geopolitical climates of North Africa specifically but also the wider MENA region. She is fluent in French and Arabic.

name: Zoë Cousins
paragraphs: Zoë has extensive experience with sports-related corruption matters and investigations in support of class action litigation. She is a member of the Association of British Investigators, holds a certification in Mastercard's Ciphertrace software, and is a PRINCE2 practitioner.
